title: feat: auto-deactivate unstable community models
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Adds `disabledAt`/`disabledReason`/`disabledBy` to `communityEndpoint` (D1 migration 0034)
- `gen.pollinations.ai`: disabled models are hidden from `/models` and rejected with a clear 400 + reason at request time
- `enter.pollinations.ai`: admin `deactivate`/`reactivate`/`list` routes gated by a new scoped `COMMUNITY_MONITOR_TOKEN`, plus an owner-facing `reactivate` route
- My Models dashboard: disabled banner with reason + reactivate button
- Tests for both services (gen + enter), all passing

Reactivation is manual-only (owner dashboard or maintainer) — nothing auto-reactivates. This unblocks a follow-up health-monitor loop (tracked separately) that will flag and deactivate genuinely unstable models with a specific reason.

## Test plan
- [x] `enter.pollinations.ai`: `npx vitest run test/community-endpoint-admin.test.ts` (7 tests)
- [x] `gen.pollinations.ai`: `npx vitest run src/community-endpoints.test.ts test/tracking-observability.test.ts` (24 tests)
- [x] Biome check clean
- [ ] Apply migration 0034 to staging + prod D1 after merge

gist.headline: Nap time for wobbly models
gist.blurb: We've added a new health monitoring system for community models. If a model gets a little unstable, it'll take a quick nap until its owner can check on it and click reactivate.
gist.summary: Added disabledAt and disabledReason fields to community endpoints, allowing unstable models to be automatically hidden from /models and temporarily deactivated with a clear 400 error.
gist.impact: Model owners will now see a disabled banner with the specific reason and a 'reactivate' button in their dashboard if their community model goes offline or becomes unstable.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a caretaker or doctor checking on a 'sleeping' or resting server/code-plant. A clipboard or dashboard showing health status.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny doctor's stethoscope, checking on a sleeping retro server covered in soft vines. A chunky pixel robot with a monitor head stands nearby holding a clipboard with a green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
